hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The value is a documentation field required only in non-Python environments to explain how Unicode escapes (`\uXXXX`) are defined and applied, with no literal runtime value in the JSON output.
The literal string is `\u` (backslash followed by lowercase u), representing the six-character sequence used in ISO 6429 to denote four hexadecimal digits.

context_pointers.vague_reference: handles the four modes specified in the serialization flags
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Refers to compact, minimum, medium, and full JSON serialization modes enforced in the rule, though the specific short-hand strings for these modes are not enumerated in C016 or elsewhere.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_parse_structured_json.json.description: Parses complete JSON objects and arrays into typed, inspectable values while preserving nested structure and member names.

feature2_parse_strings_and_escapes.json.description: Parses JSON string escapes, escaped slashes, and Unicode escape sequences into their character values.

feature3_parse_numbers_and_scalars.json.description: Parses top-level JSON scalar values and numeric forms with the resulting value category exposed in output.

feature4_reject_invalid_json.json.description: Rejects malformed or unsupported JSON input and reports a normalized parse error instead of returning partial data.

feature5_serialize_values.json.description: Serializes typed in-memory values to JSON text, including nulls, strings, lists, integers, booleans, and escaped characters.

feature6_serialize_double_handling.json.description: Serializes finite floating-point values, applies requested output precision, and normalizes errors for non-finite values when special-number output is disabled.

feature7_serialize_double_precision.json.description: Serializes a floating-point value with caller-selected significant precision settings.

cases.expected_output:
precision=1 json=0.1
precision=2 json=0.12
precision=4 json=0.1235
precision=14 json=0.12345678

feature8_serialize_indentation_modes.jso.description: Serializes the same structured value using compact, minimum, medium, and full whitespace layouts without changing the data.

feature9_serialize_special_numbers.json.description: When explicitly allowed, serializes non-finite numeric values using JSON-compatible special-number tokens.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a valid JSON string representing an object or array
_constraints.output_rule: Returns success status with type and normalized tree representation

_constraints.input_condition: Input JSON object contains string values
_constraints.output_rule: Strings must be rendered as `string:<escaped text>` in stdout

_constraints.input_condition: Input JSON object contains nested structures
_constraints.output_rule: Nested arrays and objects must preserve structure and member names recursively

_constraints.input_condition: Input JSON string contains escaped characters
_constraints.output_rule: Escaped characters (slashes, control chars) become literal values; Unicode escapes `\uXXXX` are rendered as `ꪪ` in output string

_constraints.input_condition: Input JSON string contains non-printable ASCII
_constraints.output_rule: Non-printable ASCII must be rendered as neutral backslash escapes in stdout

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a valid unsigned integer string
Input is a valid float/decimal string

_constraints.output_rule: Output must be exactly `ok=false` followed by `error=parse_error`

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a typed value descriptor with `kind='null'`
_constraints.output_rule: Serialized JSON text must be exactly `null`

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a typed value descriptor with `kind='map'` or `kind='list'`
_constraints.output_rule: Map/object arrays must be serialized using JSON object/array syntax
Non-ASCII characters must be emitted as `\uXXXX`

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a valid finite double
Input is a non-finite number (NaN, Infinity) and special-number output is disabled
Input is a valid precision request for a finite double
Input contains a list of precision settings
_constraints.output_rule: Produces one line per precision: `precision=<n> json=<value>`
Serializes using all four modes in exact order: compact, minimum, medium, full
Each mode output must be wrapped by `mode=<name>` and `--end--`

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a special number (NaN, Infinity, -Infinity) and opt-in mode is enabled
